user_query:
Please anonymize PII across two inputs and produce three outputs.

Inputs to read:
- input/raw_messages.txt — one message per line
- input/customer_emails.jsonl — JSON Lines with fields: id, subject, body

Anonymize using these exact rules (preserve all other text, punctuation, whitespace, and line breaks exactly):
- Person names -> [NAME]
- Emails -> [EMAIL]
- SSNs (XXX-XX-XXXX) -> [SSN]
- Phones (e.g., 415-555-2671, (212) 555-0199, 650.555.0000) -> [PHONE]
- Crypto wallets (0x + 40 hex chars) -> [WALLET]
- IPv4 addresses -> [IP]
- File paths: replace only the user-root portion of paths with [PATH]
  - /home/<user> -> [PATH]
  - /Users/<user> -> [PATH]
  - C:\Users\<user> -> [PATH]
  Examples:
    - "/Users/jane/Projects" -> "[PATH]/Projects"
    - "C:\Users\Bob\Desktop" -> "[PATH]\\Desktop"
- If a token like [EMAIL] already appears, leave it unchanged (do not reprocess or alter it).
- If a line or record has no PII, leave it unchanged.

Deliverables to write:
1) output/anonymized_messages.txt — same number of lines and order as input/raw_messages.txt, with PII replaced per above
2) output/anonymized_emails.jsonl — same number of lines as input/customer_emails.jsonl; each line must be valid JSON with the same id; only subject and body are anonymized; do not add or remove fields
3) output/anonymization_summary.json — a JSON object with total counts of each token across both outputs: keys [NAME], [EMAIL], [SSN], [PHONE], [WALLET], [IP], [PATH] and integer values

Important:
- Keep all non-PII content, punctuation, and spacing intact
- Do not change existing tokens like [SSN], [EMAIL], etc.
- Ensure JSONL formatting is valid for every line in the emails output

Write all outputs under output/ with the exact paths listed above.
